Transcrição Completa: I Asked Russell Brunson to Share His Sales Secrets...He Actually Did!

[00:00] I want to take a couple of questions. We've got a mic back there. All right. Tell us your name and what you sell.

[00:06] What's up, Russell and Jeremy? I sell uh Gen Z coaching, remote sales, how to get in remote sales.

[00:12] You were trying to help those guys who are lost, fatherless. How would you frame their internal beliefs and set them up for the offer, for the pitch to connect with that specific Gen Z man that's like lost?

[00:23] Where where are most of these people come? Like where are they when they're coming to your webinar, your presentation, whatever? Like like where they at in their life at that point? Are they in college? They dropouts? Are they school?

[00:35] Most of them are starting college, you know, they're told they need to go to college, get a good job, get a safe job, and they're afraid to make the jump. They're afraid of what other people think of them. They haven't found their identity yet.

[00:48] You know, they see all these hypy offers, you know, these drop shipping, crypto, all this stuff. So, they've been let down a lot because they've bought these courses and haven't gotten the results yet.

[01:00] Uh, how many of those offers did you go through as you were on your way? A lot.

[01:05] I mean, the rally is is you're coming back to you're finding where you're at, right? So, it's not like there's a magic trick other than just the empathy of what they they went through.

[01:16] And so, for you, it's like and I'd even show like if you have receipts of the stuff like I spent $397 on this course over here, went through it, nothing. I tried this over here, tried this over here, tried this, you know, like, and you're showing the same path that they're on right now.

[01:34] Um, and then what people are looking for is hope, right? So, it's like I was I was stuck. I was frustrated, things like that. Like, um, but then like whatever your breakthrough was, like you're sharing that breakthrough and then on the other side of that, like this is this is what's looking like, right? This is what life looks like now on this side of it. And then you got to paint a picture of of that, right?

[01:57] So, with you, um, you're saying a lot of these guys, they you saying they don't have fathers or their fathers aren't present in their life or what was

[02:05] Yeah. Most of the time they don't have strong father figures that kind of can help guide them and lead them personally.

[02:11] And you're talking about like on a webinar. Yeah. Okay.

[02:14] So, let's say you have a thousand people on there. It's just how you how you uh you know, when they're on there like, okay, you know, you guys are all high level. I mean, you guys are smart. I mean, you know, you wouldn't be on here like you know, and as a smart, you know, do you say Jenz is it just marketed as a Gen Z thing?

[02:32] I don't know. I do I do market Gen Z. Yeah. Okay.

[02:35] So, you could even type you could even go that I mean you if there's a bunch of other people in like millennials and stuff, you wouldn't want to say this, but if it's all Gen Z, like hey, I mean you guys are smart. I mean, you guys are Gen Z. You're you're the you're the you're the generation that's smart and you're the generation that knows because you're smart, because you're high level that the greatest way to go to have wealth is obviously I mean you know not to go to college because look what happened with your parents that might have went to college but the smartest way the smartest way as you know as an entrepreneur is to learn XYZ and to be able to start your own business and to have time freedom.

[03:13] I mean you'd be surprised you know you guys are different. I mean, you're not like all the other average people out there that think that, you know, they're going to get rich by going to school, spending a couple hundred grand, you know?

[03:24] So, I can start framing that as I go throughout the car. There's a lot more to there, but you can start framing them.

[03:30] And I always say like I hate when sales people like it always drove me nuts when I was in sales and people like, "Oh, my leads aren't qualified or like my leads just they're they're I'm trying to qualify my leads." I'm like, "Stop qualifying the damn leads. Build them into the person. Build them into the identity of the person who is qualified to buy.

[03:50] Unless you're selling a $10 million offer to a homeless person, they can probably find the funding. It's not like the money doesn't exist. It's just a priority of where they're currently spending that money. And until you learn that, it's going to remain a priority of where they're currently spending it rather than getting them over here where they feel like whatever you're offering is more of a priority for that money.

[04:13] So, it's just building them into the identity of the person who becomes the buyer. That's all you have to do. Stop the qualifi. I don't like the qualifying thing. I think anybody anybody that shows up is qualified. They can find the funding if you build them into that identity of the person who goes out and finds the funding.

[04:30] And you think about their whole life they've heard the opposite, right? And so they've heard they're not good enough, they're not smart enough, drop out of school. It's like that's the message they've heard and they they don't like it. They're rejecting it. So you're coming in as a separate voice.

[04:44] It's uh Kennedy talked about this in Funnel Hacking Live two years ago. He said he's like in every in every room he's like you look at it, there's a room of people and they're following somebody listening. And he's like, he's like, in every room, there's like a third of the people that are cool with the status quo. They love it. They believe it's correct. There's a third that's like, uh, you know, they're okay. They're too lazy to do anything.

[05:08] He's like, there's a third of the room in any room that know that something's not right. They know this is not the way things are supposed to be. There's no other option. And he said they're literally waiting for the heretic to arrive. That's what Dan says.

[05:22] I love when he said that cuz like that's that's what we're waiting for. They're waiting for someone to step up and be like, "No, you're not wrong. This is not your fault. This is something else." Right? And I think that's all the message or all the identity stuff you talked about there, right?

[05:36] like you're coming in like you're the smart generation. You're not the dumb ones. You're not, you know, like like again because you're not like everybody else that's not here, you know? It's like this room is different. It's your bu

[05:47] And look, a lot of people are like, "Well, this is manipulated." No, I'm doing this for them. Yeah. Because if I and I I learned this from from Tony. I think he's one of the greatest. Like if Tony like couldn't get them out of their limiting beliefs, they would never change. Like everything would be the same for them.

[06:06] So you're doing it for them. And these skill sets Russ was talking about like these are things you do for people to help them overcome these stories or limiting beliefs that have held them back. They've already done that. So if you buy into their story about why they can't buy, you're not offering them any value. You wasted their time.

[06:25] So it's like you're doing this for them. It's like a patient like you know I went to school to become a psychologist and you're taught let's say if somebody wants to end their life well if I don't learn techniques or how to emotionally connect with them to take them out of those belief systems they might actually follow through and do that if I just tell them why they shouldn't that goes in one ear out the other if I try to persuade them.

[06:48] So that's where I had to reverse engineer and get them out of that identity into the identity of the person that would never want to do that. So it's it's the same concept because if you don't sell to them, you're not solving their problems. You're not really helping them get where they want.

[07:03] Let's take let's take one or two more questions. Who else we got here? Couple. Yeah.

[07:08] So um I'm an owner of an event called Halloween Town. And so I've been operating for six years. I'm getting ready to launch a franchise. Okay. So that's what I'm into.

[07:19] And so the question I have for you, Russell, I had to write this down. Let me pull this up real quick. Halloween Town, like is it like the Halloween time? You go in and buy all the costumes and stuff.

[07:29] No, so that's It's funny because everybody's been saying that. I might have to change the name. Uh that's uh Spirit uh Halloween Spirit. So Halloween Town is a pumpkin patch and it's for kids uh two to eight years old. So we do carnival, pumpkins, just just fun stuff for kids like a Disneyland.

[07:49] So, um, the question is because I'm kind of like obviously I know the business, but now I'm launching something new. And so, if I, if you had to start from zero tomorrow, no list, no audience, and let's say you had $1,000 bucks, what exact steps would you take in the next 30 days?

[08:06] Well, first off, congratulations because selling a business opportunity is the easiest and the best thing to sell on the planet. Um me and Dan Kenny have a book call coming out really soon called Selling Opportunity which is all about like how to take whatever you're selling and turning it into an opportunity because um when you're selling a business you're selling money at a discount. So it's like the easiest thing to sell. It's the best thing to sell.

[08:30] So I I would think that's easier to sell than trying to get people to to kids to come to an actual you know pumpkin patch. Like that seems easier. So um so that's exciting for me for you. Um thank you.

[08:42] But I think I mean my my playbook's pretty simple. You know what I mean? Like step number one is like how do we craft an offer? And I'm not sure are you are you doing franchise for sure? You going to do licensing? Have you decided the path?

[08:55] Yeah. So, uh franchising for sure. I did look at licensing, but I've partnered with someone that's very very good at franchising. So, I'm just going to go that route because you can kind of get into some messy stuff down the road. I've heard.

[09:08] Um so, I mean, and then I know there's franchise law with stuff some stuff you can and can't say. So, putting it through the lens of like I have no idea what franchise law actually is. I would still come back and like how do you create a really really good offer, right?

[09:22] I'm assuming with the business you have right now, do you how many locations? Do you have one location, a couple locations?

[09:28] We have uh two in Phoenix and four in Las Vegas.

[09:31] What's the average round revenue that someone would make if they were running one?

[09:35] Uh profit or gross? Uh profit? Uh probably around $150 to $200k.

[09:41] Does someone have to have an existing pumpkin patch or is this anybody can

[09:45] Anybody you can have an existing pumpkin patch. You could own a piece of land. You could never have done this ever in your life.

[09:51] And this is all just happening in the Halloween season. So, Yep. Yeah. And that's the I think that's the attraction. That's what pulls people in is the ability to be able to do something that's seasonal and and then just not have to do it.

[10:06] So, it's like just remember, you're not selling them a franchise. You're selling them the results of what the franchise does for them. Have more time. have your own business, make more money. You're selling the results of the franchise, not the franchise itself. Yeah.

[10:21] And that'd be it. I mean, I'd be I'd be creating create an offer. For me personally, I would write a presentation because that's the easiest thing. And then I would even spend that thousand bucks. I other than the Click Funnels account so you can post the pages, but other than that, um I I I would just Yeah. do some reals. Yeah.

[10:40] Yeah, I mean there's organic traffic, but also the same like everyone's default when they start a business is to like go and buy Facebook ads or things like that, which is a in theory a great thing, but like we didn't buy our first ad for ClickFunnels for two years. Like we were over $10 million in sales before we invested in our first ad.

[10:58] Like there's just better ways to get traffic. There's easier ways. Like I'd be looking for partnerships. Like who are other people that have not so much like a franchise, but they they have, you know, like the the the kind of person in my mind who would who would do something like that.

[11:13] Like there's I can think of a hundred different industries. Like Joe McCall teaches people how to buy land. Like just buy dirt, pieces of dirt. Like he'd be a great person to do a thing with. Like you just got your guy to buy some dirt, now throw some pumpkins on it, right?

[11:28] There could be another industry where it could be, you know, um homesteaders who are doing their own homestead. Like that's a whole market with a huge audience. There could be um homeschooling families. Like there's a whole huge corporate executives work a lot of hours, maybe make six figures, but work a ton of hours. Maybe they want their own thing. That would be good.

[11:48] you know, find somebody has a big list of corporate executives, partner with them, like an affiliate deal. Yeah, there's so many there's so many of those in that I would just go out and like try to get on those podcast, get on their email list, doing a webinar with them, like just I would be I'd be pounding the payment and save your thousand bucks and just get those because those you only pay after the sale's been made, you know, and it's it's unique content for the content creator because it's an interesting business. It's different.

[12:15] You know, most content creators are looking for content and so if you come in like, "Hey, um, I'm helping people start businesses where they throw some pumpkins and make a yearly income in 30 days. Uh, can I talk about your podcast?" Like, that's unique and interesting. Yeah.

[12:30] Like I'll even pay you 20% of the of the franchise fees, whatever. Like, like you can get deals like that all day long. Um, and I would do that before I went and gave Zuckerberg money because he's smart enough. He knows how to get all of our money in every campaign eventually.

[13:15] I mean there's people that Do they franchise events? So, do you want me to give you a really shortcut to success? Sure.

[13:22] You need to go find that person and hire them. Okay. So, you don't So you can learn from their mistakes so you don't make the same mistakes themselves. Unless you want to spend a decade going through trial and error.

[13:35] The quickest way to success. Smartest people in the world don't learn from their mistakes. They learn from other people's mistakes so they don't make the same mistakes themselves. You're right. Instead of going through a 10-year learning group, that's what I'd do. I'd go borrow funds. I'd hire the person who's mastered the thing you want to do and go hire that person and learn the ins and outs because what's the biggest reason why a business fails? Because they don't know what they're doing, right? And they're kind of guessing. But if you know exactly what to do, it's kind of hard to fail.

[14:05] So, can I just in addition to that last question real quick because we got you got five people behind you. They're like getting they're like starting to look angry back there. So, so you you've built a massive following and a loyal community. Uh, what's the one thing you did that most people overlook when building like a tribe or a following?

[14:24] I cared a lot. Most people are doing it because they want to build a tribe. Like I legitimately love my people and like legitimately care legitimately. Like, you know, I I had an exit four years ago. I'd be independently rich for the rest of my life. And I said no because like I wanted to serve these people. Like that's that's I think the difference is people are doing it for as a marketing tactic versus like people who are like I'm actually obsessed with my people and I want them to be successful. People can tell. It's it's a weird it's a weird hack, but it's the like that's actually the truth.

[14:58] Let's take uh let's take one maybe two more. All right, Russell. Um I'm curious to know what you think um the impact of LLMs will be on ClickFunnels or things of that nature moving forward. Like how do we prepare or adapt?

[15:13] Yeah, it's very it's very interesting problem to solve. we're all I think a lot of us are all playing with and figuring it out. Um, I think I think the thing that's most exciting for me is because funnels are like right now they are a thing. There's pages, but funnels is a philosophy, right? Like you're taking somebody.

[15:31] I think what gets me most excited about the future and like where things are going is um I always talk about like one to many is hard because you can't talk to each person individually, but with AI we actually can, right?

[15:42] Like right now we're doing um some tests with um I have a personality profiling company. So we take personality profiles and um we're testing now where someone will come in and they'll they'll come into one of our pages and we can rewrite the page on the fly based on who they are. Like hey this person's anagram three whatever they're a high D low I like write the headline for something like that. Rewrite this thing.

[16:08] So like we can start changing the pages based on who the people are when we know about them. And I think that that'll continue to go but you know I don't think it'll be pages in two years from now. that's going to be but it's still conversations right so funnels the psychology of funnels won't change the the mechanism what they look like might be different and I think the power behind them will be a lot better because we'll be able to literally yeah it comes back to the principles like we talked about right

[16:35] and so for me like those are the questions we're asking a lot of companies like what does this look like what's the front of it how do we get you know how so I don't know exactly what it looks like but those are the things I think that are powerful about is just no longer are we talking to traffic we get to talk to like the specific person based on who they are what they believe and it's

[16:54] Um, it's it's fascinating. We're seeing right now just in customer support, like our customer support AI agents are beating humans half the time. They're saving more sales, increasing. It's like it's crazy where things are getting to.

[17:07] And I think um when we start looking at like funnels aren't just here's the pages in the funnel, but it's it's you know, it could be chat bots, could be it could, you know, whatever that it it morphs into in the next little bit. Uh, but the the strategies, the principles are the same. So, it's it's I think it's an exciting

📊 RESUMO EXECUTIVO

NÚMEROS PRINCIPAIS:

$397 - Exemplo de curso comprado sem resultados

$10 milhões - Oferta hipotética para homeless

$1,000 - Orçamento inicial proposto para começar

$150-200k - Lucro anual por localização de franquia

$10 milhões+ - Faturamento do ClickFunnels antes do primeiro anúncio pago

2 anos - Tempo antes de investir em anúncios pagos

2 localizações em Phoenix

4 localizações em Las Vegas

6 anos - Tempo de operação do Halloween Town

20% - Sugestão de comissão de afiliado

1/3 - Proporção de pessoas esperando por mudança

4 anos atrás - Momento da oferta de exit de Russell

ESTRATÉGIAS E SISTEMAS:

Construção de Identidade do Comprador:

Não qualificar leads, mas construir a identidade de quem compra

Mostrar empatia pelo caminho percorrido

Compartilhar histórias de fracassos semelhantes

Pintar visão do futuro após transformação

Framework de Vendas:

Foco em vender resultados, não produtos

Vender dinheiro com desconto (oportunidades de negócio)

Superar crenças limitantes do prospect

Fazer pelo cliente, não manipular

Estratégia de Tráfego Orgânico:

Parcerias com listas existentes

Podcasts e webinars conjuntos

Afiliados pagos apenas após venda

Evitar anúncios pagos inicialmente

Nichos Sugeridos para Halloween Town:

Compradores de terrenos (Joe McCall)

Homesteaders

Famílias homeschooling

Executivos corporativos buscando negócio próprio

Construção de Comunidade:

Cuidado genuíno com a audiência

Não fazer por tática de marketing

Obsessão real pelo sucesso das pessoas

ESTRUTURA:

Filosofia de Vendas: "Querer o negócio mas não precisar dele"

Regra de Kennedy: 1/3 das pessoas em qualquer sala estão esperando pelo "herético" que desafia o status quo

Princípio Tony Robbins: Ajudar pessoas a superar crenças limitantes

Funil como Filosofia: Não apenas páginas, mas conversas guiadas

IA e Personalização: Páginas dinâmicas baseadas em perfis de personalidade

DADOS FINANCEIROS:

Franquia Halloween Town: $150-200k lucro anual por localização

ClickFunnels: $10M+ faturamento antes de anúncios pagos

Investimento inicial sugerido: $1,000 (apenas ClickFunnels)

Comissão de afiliado sugerida: 20% das taxas de franquia

DURAÇÃO: ~17 minutos FORMATO: Sessão de perguntas e respostas ao vivo com Russell Brunson e Jeremy
